fix: center auth logo horizontally

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(d1): replace UNIQUE indexes with partial index excluding cancelled rows

Root cause: cancelled reservations still blocked the UNIQUE constraint
idx_res_unique(tenant_id, staff_id, start_at) and idx_resv_unique(tenant_id,
staff_id, start_at, end_at). The /slots API and /reserve overlap check both
filter by status != 'cancelled', showing slots as available. But the D1
INSERT hit the UNIQUE constraint from the cancelled row → duplicate_slot.

Fix: Migration 0014 drops both idx_res_unique and idx_resv_unique, replaces
with idx_res_unique_active — a partial unique index with
WHERE status != 'cancelled'. This allows re-booking cancelled time slots
while still preventing true double-bookings of active reservations.

D1 migration applied to production (saas-factory-staging): verified.

E2E verified:
- Previously-cancelled 09:00 slot → 200 OK (was 409 duplicate_slot)
- Full slot (06:00, both staff busy) → 409 all_staff_busy
- /slots ○/× matches /reserve accept/reject exactly
- Cross-tenant isolation → correct

panel — textarea now stays editable during DM1 loading, only the Act
button locks. Restores intended UX from BUG-007 Won't Fix decision.

Railway logs will pinpoint exactly which step fails. API failures now
log to dm_logs with NULL dm_response (parity with DM1 failure path).

Rules Arbiter failures. Client routes dm2_error to rulesLog feed,
keeping DM1 and DM2 error messages in their correct panels. Added
error-tag display in the rules feed for parity with narrative feed.

BUG-012: session_resumed handler now checks for empty history before
adding the resume divider. If both narrative and rules history are
empty, falls back to the new-session intro message rather than showing
an orphaned "Session resumed" divider with no content above it.

feat(compiler): validate entity names match createDb() model registry (#984)

* feat(compiler): add DatabaseAnalyzer to validate entity-model registration [#969]

Add compile-time validation that entity names match createDb() model
registry keys. Introduces DatabaseAnalyzer that detects createDb() calls
from @vertz/db and extracts model keys, then CompletenessValidator
cross-references entity names against registered models.

- New DatabaseIR type and DatabaseAnalyzer
- ENTITY_MODEL_NOT_REGISTERED diagnostic with actionable suggestion
- Skips validation when no createDb() calls found (plain EntityDbAdapter)
- Wired into compiler pipeline and exported from package

Closes #969

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(compiler): handle shorthand, variable refs, spread, computed keys in DatabaseAnalyzer [#969]

Address adversarial review findings:
- Handle shorthand `models` property (e.g., `createDb({ models })`)
- Resolve variable references (e.g., `models: myModels`)
- Emit warning for spread assignments (cannot statically resolve)
- Emit warning for computed property names (cannot statically resolve)
- Lazy-compute registeredList in validator (only when needed)
- Handle empty models object edge case
- Add tests for all new patterns

commit.message:
Fix SIGSEGV in audio IO thread and stuck Transcribing state

Two bugs fixed:

SIGSEGV (libffi use-after-free in CoreAudio IO thread):
The audio callback is a libffi closure whose lifetime is tied to the
sounddevice InputStream Python object.  The old stop_recording() called
stop() + close() + stream = None in sequence, which could free the closure
while the CoreAudio IO thread was still mid-callback, producing a SIGSEGV
at 0x4 (null + offsetof closure field).

Fix: stop_recording() now calls abort() for immediate halt but does NOT
close or release the stream object.  The stream is closed lazily at the
start of the next start_recording() call (safe because the IO thread has
fully exited by then) or on explicit shutdown via the new close() method.
TranscriptionManager.shutdown() now calls recorder.close() accordingly.

Stuck "Transcribing..." (queue backlog):
TranscriberWorker processes jobs serially; Whisper is slow.  With many
transmissions the queue grew without bound and all entries stayed stuck
at "Transcribing..." indefinitely.

Fix: enqueue() now drops jobs once the queue reaches _MAX_QUEUE_DEPTH (5)
and logs a warning.  _process() truncates clips longer than _MAX_AUDIO_SECS
(60 s) before noise reduction and Whisper, capping per-job latency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor: split monolithic indexer into Block / Token / Contract / Internal Tx

Break the 1114-line index.ts into focused modules:
- block.ts — blocks, txs, accounts, receipts/events, balance refresh
- token.ts — ERC-20/721/1155 transfer detection, metadata, balances
- contract.ts — contract creation, code_hash (SHA-256), account upsert
- internal-tx.ts — debug_traceTransaction callTracer → internal_transactions
- state.ts — indexer_state helpers, daily stats, admin commands
- index.ts — thin orchestrator (pipeline: block → token+contract → internal-tx)

Token and contract processors run in parallel per block. Internal tx
tracing is graceful — skipped silently if debug API is unavailable.

Adds internal_transactions table migration and TraceCall type.

rename dropbear to sillybear throughout the codebase

Global rename: DROPBEAR→SILLYBEAR, Dropbear→Sillybear, dropbear→sillybear
in all source files, headers, build system, manpages, tests, docs,
and vendored library bridge headers.

Renamed files:
- src/dropbearkey.c → src/sillybearkey.c
- src/dropbearconvert.c → src/sillybearconvert.c
- src/dropbear_lint.sh → src/sillybear_lint.sh
- manpages/dropbear.8 → manpages/sillybear.8
- manpages/dropbearkey.1 → manpages/sillybearkey.1
- manpages/dropbearconvert.1 → manpages/sillybearconvert.1
- libtomcrypt/src/headers/tomcrypt_dropbear.h → tomcrypt_sillybear.h
- test/test_dropbear.py → test/test_sillybear.py
- test/test_dropbearconvert.py → test/test_sillybearconvert.py
- test/parent_dropbear_map.py → test/parent_sillybear_map.py
- debian/dropbear.* → debian/sillybear.*

Output binaries: sillybear, sillybearkey, sillybearconvert, dbclient.
SSH ident string: SSH-2.0-sillybear_<version>.

commit.message:
Persist GeoIP cache across app launches to avoid rate limiting

In-memory cache was cleared on every restart, causing all IPs to be
re-requested each test session and exhausting provider rate limits.
Cache is now saved to UserDefaults and restored on init so each IP
is only ever looked up once per device.

Fix: allow microphone access in Permissions-Policy header

microphone=() was blocking all microphone access at the HTTP level,
overriding Chrome's site permission. Changed to microphone=(self) to
allow our own origin while still blocking third-party iframes.

commit.message:
fix: unblock service worker — resolve stale cache hydration crash

Middleware was blocking /sw.js (redirecting to login), preventing the
browser's SW update check from succeeding. Old SW kept serving stale
JS chunks, causing originalFactory.call webpack crash on /orders and
/sales. Added cleanup SW that clears all caches on activate, excluded
sw.js from auth middleware, and added JS-side unregister on app load.

commit.message:
feat(marketplace+fluxos): seletores de quantidade + 8 novos nós no Designer de Fluxos (#23)

Marketplace (Faturamento):
- Agentes de IA Extra: stepper −/+, quantidade × R$ 197/mês, compra múltipla
- Números WhatsApp Extra: stepper −/+, quantidade × R$ 97/mês, compra múltipla
- Nova seção: Identificar Objeção por IA — R$ 97/mês (sem stepper, ativação única)
- requestAddonPurchase insere múltiplas rows no pedidos_creditos

Designer de Fluxos — 8 novos nós:
- 🏢 Transferir Departamento (nome + número destino)
- 📋 Captura de Dados (nome, sobrenome, telefone, motivo)
- 🔀 Condição IF/ELSE (campo + valor, 2 saídas)
- ⏳ Aguardar / Wait (tempo configurável: min/h/dias)
- 📤 Follow-up (mensagem pré-definida + delay + variáveis)
- 🧠 Contextualizar Conversa (histórico + dados + objeções)
- 🗂️ Mover no CRM (select do estágio do pipeline)
- 🛡️ Identificar Objeção IA — recurso pago (bloqueado até compra no Marketplace)

Acesso controlado:
- _fluxoObjecao bool gateado por addon_objecao ativo no Supabase
- palette-objecao fica locked com cursor:not-allowed até contratar
- dragObjecao() alerta e redireciona para Marketplace se não comprado
